fields.description:
# Exemplar remediation: llm/plan_review/ end-to-end + enable BLE001/T201 with shrinking allowlist

The reference PR. Its diff IS the convention — every other batch copies it. Do NOT parallelize this with
the sweeps; it must land (and lock the lint) before they start. Depends on the logging substrate.

## What
- Remediate every broad-except in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` to the observability floor: log with
  `exc_info` OR carry a justified `# noqa: BLE001` (reason string required). Opportunistic narrowing only
  where a specific type is genuinely expected, with a test.
- Migrate `print(..., file=sys.stderr)` diagnostics in the package to the named logger.
- Enable ruff **BLE001** and **T201** (scoped) in `pyproject.toml` `[tool.ruff.lint]`, gated behind a
  `per-file-ignores` allowlist that exempts every not-yet-swept package (clone the shrinking-allowlist
  pattern from `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt`); `llm/plan_review/` is NOT in the allowlist.
- T201 scoping (corrected per plan-review G4): banned in library/core/MCP, but a `per-file-ignore` is needed
  for the legitimate-`print` surfaces — BOTH (a) the CLI presentation layer (`_cli`, `_commands`), where
  `print(json.dumps(...))` stdout output is the machine contract, AND (b) the reconciler subprocess
  (`_engine/rebar_reconciler/`), which legitimately prints operational `RECON:`/`ERROR:` lines to its OWN
  stderr (all 42 existing `# noqa: T201` sites live there, NOT in the CLI).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All broad-except sites in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` meet the observability floor; no unjustified
      `except ...: pass` remains.
- [ ] Stderr-diagnostic prints in the package are migrated to `logging.getLogger(__name__)`.
- [ ] `pyproject.toml` enables `BLE001` + `T201` with a `per-file-ignores` allowlist covering all un-swept
      packages; CI is green.
- [ ] `llm/plan_review/` is excluded from the allowlist (it is fully conformant).
- [ ] `make lint` + full test suite pass.
